fields.description: Git→FUSE propagation: detect upstream commits not yet visible in the workspace FUSE mount, run monitor/scripts/sync-workspace-step4c.js to propagate, commit + push the resulting sentinel. Companion to dome-workspace-sync (FUSE→git direction). PROP-074 architectural separation.
fields.model: Haiku (per directive Q2 pre-baked answer; reliability watch — flag for operator if Haiku fails reliably)
fields.skill_md_content:
---
name: dome-mirror
description: Git→FUSE propagation — detect upstream commits not yet in workspace FUSE mount, propagate via sync-workspace-step4c.js helper, commit + push the resulting sentinel.
---

Read your full instructions from the prompt file at `monitor/prompts/dome-mirror.md` in the "dome-model-review" folder in your workspace. Follow those instructions exactly — they contain your complete procedure, output schemas, and rules.

Your workspace contains the "dome-model-review" folder with all agent prompts, outputs, and configuration. Start by reading the dome-mirror prompt file, then execute it.

operator_action_required.scheduling_offset_recommendation: After creating dome-mirror with cron `0 * * * *`, consider updating dome-workspace-sync's cron to `5 * * * *` so dome-mirror updates FUSE BEFORE workspace-sync compares FUSE vs clone. This prevents the mtime-guard backfire where workspace-sync would otherwise revert recent commits (smart_copy would compare stale FUSE vs fresh clone and SKIP the copy under 'anti-reversion; FUSE matches historic commit' — same data, opposite direction).

post_deploy_validation_plan.metric_to_watch: Count of new sync-workspace-runs-*.json files per 24h window in monitor/integrity/
post_deploy_validation_plan.prior_baseline: ~14% sentinel rate under PROP-073 (1 sentinel per ~7 cycles)
post_deploy_validation_plan.if_below_target: Flag for operator review — may indicate Haiku also has reliability ceiling for this single-action prompt (escalate to Sonnet)
post_deploy_validation_plan.workspace_sync_regression_check: Verify workspace-sync.md's FUSE→git half still runs reliably after simplification. Past rate: ~100% (run-reports written every cycle). Should remain so.

lessons_captured: Agent self-conception filters out unfamiliar actions regardless of prompt design — extending a multi-purpose agent's responsibilities beyond its established identity triggers pattern-rejection failure modes NOT addressable via prompt design alone.

FIRST + LAST positions execute reliably across Sonnet/Haiku; MIDDLE positions drop — empirically established by PROP-072 but did not generalize to 'put new work at FIRST position' because pattern-rejection still fired against actions inconsistent with agent's established role.

Self-attestation backstops are gameable (the same pattern-rejection that causes the skip causes the agent to not notice it skipped). Detective signal requires structural evidence (sentinel files, commit titles, file mtimes), not LLM self-report.
Stage-delta detection (PROP-073 sub-fix #1) DID work for preventing mis-titled commits. Defense-in-depth detection layers DO work; preventive prompt-design fixes for pattern-rejection do not.

Architectural separation into single-purpose agents is the structural fix when extending a multi-purpose agent triggers pattern-rejection. Cost (one more scheduled task slot, one more hourly cycle of clone + push) is acceptable when the alternative is permanent unreliability.
